Discussion Topic

Part 1:


     • Post your video lesson from Sunday.

     • Review 2 classmates’ videos.

     • Discuss 3 positive aspects of their video.

     • Give one area of improvement


Part 2:

TEFL or TESOL Jobs


     • Research a website that will help you to find a job

     • Why is the website helpful?

     • Include the URL


***OPTIONAL:     


     • Do you know of any available jobs?      

     • Any connections or volunteer opportunities you want to share with the class?


Discussion Thread

Tina Liu

Mar 6, 2021 6:20pm


Hi everybody,


Wow, I finally got the first posting spot lol… a first only 2 weeks left for me in the program lol… Here’s my video lesson, 2021 Space Odyssey – A Lesson on the Colors of the Rainbow, done on a free Virtual Reality platform called AltspaceVR. My husband enjoys making computer generated 3-D models, and was able to get a ready-made model asset of the International Space Station, for me to teach my lesson on the colors of the rainbow, in virtual outer space all suited up, and also inside!


I chose to make my avatar as a blonde and blue-eyed Caucasian, to address the teacher preferential hiring practice for them in Taiwan, a job search challenge I will be facing but also a business opportunity to target, when I’ll move there in two or three months. Here’s a demo video and paper I did the Culture course that speaks more about this preferential hiring practice and the solutions teaching in VR environments can provide.

I should seek clarification on what the time allotment means on the lesson plan, Because five to ten minutes seems too short, for a complete lesson with students participation. Mine would probably be a whole hour, that I showed only highlights of, and even only that, my video is 16 minutes (lesson plan is attached).


Fast tracking to Part Two of the discussion, this program inspired me to founding a startup with my husband to create VR classrooms with teaching materials and rent time slots to schools, teachers and students. We are offering teaching and volunteering opportunities that will be profitable beyond a fixed hourly/monthly salary, as an independent contractor or collaborating partner in our start up. I’m excited to share that we just found a great company name last week, domain, Facebook, Instagram pages, as well as hashtag #VRealTime all available still as well.  Come have a VRealTime with us!

 

Part 2:TEFL or TESOL Jobs

Research a website that will help you to find a job: Hess Education

Why is the website helpful?  Hess’s school site it’s helpful because they are the biggest TESOL school In Taiwan that also has ranches in Mainland China, Singapore, Vietnam and South Korea.  The chances of getting a job or to sell VR classroom renewal with just one application/email with this school it’s better than applying to any other individual school in Taiwan at least.

Include the URL: https://www.hesseducation.com/locations


Kelly H. Kim
Mar 7, 2021 5:47pm

Hi Tina, 
Your videos are so amazing! I’ve never seen this type of video lesson in any of the four courses of TESOL classes I took! How original and unique. It’s great you have these skills in utilizing the VR platform you’ve built with your husband. These videos must be very entertaining for the students, which means they will enjoy your lesson better and stay engaged. That’s huge especially when you target younger students to teach online because their attention span is usually shorter than adult learners. I also like how you used lots of repetitions and incorporated role playing into your lesson after introducing all the colors. As an area to improve, I recommend writing down your questions somewhere so the students can see written letters and learn as they follow along. For example, if you had written down the sentences on the board, you would have had easier time explaining how you want the students to participate in the role play activity. Overall, very cool video lesson! Thanks for sharing!


Tina Liu
Mar 12, 2021 9:45pm


Thank you for all the compliments Kelly, I enjoyed your lesson too!  I’ve answered to your comment about the video and suggestions in a post to everyone below since everyone had the same comments. If Korea has similar preferential hiring practices against East Asians, my VR classroom can offer you a solution.  We’re in need of a collaborating partner who has a business background and we welcome you to collaborate with us and be able to use the classrooms we create to teach your students.  Surely you will be the only one in Korea!  I can forward you more information on the benefits you’ll have partnering up for us, which includes representation in Korea.


By the way, I hope to have your permission to publish your kindest compliments on my company website as a testimonial. Please let me know if you have any objections. You will be able to see it soon on www.VRealTime.net
Much gratitude in advance!


Cheers… Tina

Tina,


I have no problem with you publishing my comments on your website. Regarding the partnership idea, since I haven’t switched my career to teaching yet, it might be a bit too soon for me to dive into running it here in Korea. However, if I come across an opportunity that suites your business model, I will make sure I refer you I with you luck with everything! 


Thank you Kelly!  Please do connect with me on LinkedIn or FB!  I planning a class field trip to Altspace after class ends next week.  I hope you’ll join us and best of luck to your


Gabriela Miranda Laguna (She/Her/Hers)
Mar 11, 2021 8:46pm


Hello Tina, 


Your videos are fascinating. Teaching virtually comes with its own set of challenges especially during a pandemic, and you and your idea about videos with 3-D models are amazing. These videos will be awesome for your future students. It definitely, increases attention from the students because it is easier to comprehend and captures more attention than text or static images.


I recommend you write a little more, so the students can take some notes correctly about what you are explaining.

By the way, I hope to have your permission to publish your kindest compliments on my company website as a testimonial. Please let me know if you have any objections. You will be able to see it soon on www.VRealTime.net Much gratitude in advance!

Mar 12, 2021 12:04pm

Obviously, the animation will keep students engaged – not to mention the cool outfits the Avatars have.  I also agree that having some of the sentences written out rather than just the flag with the colors might help improve the video – but honestly, this is just a great video. I think the way you spoke and listened was very patient and encouraging, and I’m sure that would boost your student’s


Tina Liu
Mar 12, 2021 10:06pm
I’m glad finally someone picked up on our music choice You must be a SCI-FI fan too Jonathan?  I’m happy too you appreciate our film making with camera angles etc… my husband had a career in TV, so I’m especially picky about that ‘I’ve answered to your comment about the video and suggestions in a post to everyone below since everyone had the same comments.  I deeply appreciate your encouraging words about my teaching style, especially coming from a teacher such as yourself since I have no formal teaching experience.  Please join us if you are ever interesting in teaching in these VR classrooms we create!  We can certainly use collaboration from someone will film experience since we have to shoot many more of these sample class videos for the startup I’m forming with my husband to sell timeshare usage of these classrooms. 
By the way, I hope to have your permission to publish your kindest compliments on my company website as a testimonial. Please let me know if you have any objections. You will be able to see it soon on www.VRealTime.net Much gratitude in advance!

Tina Liu
Mar 12, 2021 9:31pm


Thank you, Kelly, Gabriela, and Jonathan!
I deeply appreciate all of your encouraging compliments, comments, and suggestions, which helped me understand there may be some misunderstanding to these videos that appeared like animated cartoons. Since there seems to be a general consensus on that point, I will make a general response to that point and detail more on how these VR classrooms work. 


They are live and completely immersive VR environments. Each student participates in a first-person view as though they are in front of an instructor in physical reality. The ‘video’ was shot in this way to demonstrate how an actual class will operate. These videos are not cartoon animations for students to watch, they are 3D graphic representations of classroom environments, teaching materials, and of the people in them, live and in real-time.  So in essence, the students do not watch a recorded video but participate in a live 3D VR environment, which is available on VR goggles, like Oculus Quest as well as in 2D on a standard computer, both Windows and Mac.  


My husband is developing applications that will allow the instructor to instantaneously produce ‘in-world’ any 3D object that is contained in our library of teaching objects. This app will insert words and objects that match them, for example, the word ‘Apple’ right next to a 3D apple, which the teacher and students will be able to interact with, including moving them around as they would in a video game. He is also planning on creating an entire set of Cuisenaire rods, with the words already printed on them. It will also enable the students to have pens to write within the VR classroom. Tilt Brush is an example of this. 


VR has given us a whole new level of possibilities for remote teaching, including being able to teach students globally in fantastical worlds of imagination, where anything could be made possible, fully immersive and interactive without losing social contact, rather than just the one-way video conference type of remote learning. Any teacher will be able to come into our classrooms with their goggles or computer and teach their students with the material we provide or custom make for their class. I hope you will all join us and please feel free to connect with me on LinkedIn or Facebook, so I can give you a virtual tour of these classrooms to experience and know them for yourself. My contact information is  on my is bio page here on Canvas.
By the way, I hope to have your permission to publish your kindest compliments on my company website as a testimonial. Please let me know if you have any objections. You will be able to see it soon on www.VRealTime.net Much gratitude in advance!
Cheers… Tina
